# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 142
- **Lines of code**: 46,136
- **Languages**: TypeScript (129), JSON (7), Markdown (3), Other (3)
- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-08T17:29:21.902Z

## Architecture Zones

### Analysis Engine (`analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline implementing zone enrichment, context generation, callgraph findings, and AI-assisted classification.

- Files: 39
- Cohesion: 0.66, Coupling: 0.34
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph-findings.ts, src/analyzers/claude-client.ts, src/analyzers/context.ts, src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts, src/analyzers/enrich.ts (+11 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/analysis-engine/context.md`

### Branch Work Analysis (`branch-work-analysis`)

Schema foundation, branch-work tracking pipeline, risk scoring, and MCP server wiring grouped by import proximity to the core schema types.

- Files: 29
- Cohesion: 0.42, Coupling: 0.58
- Entry points: src/analyzers/branch-work-classifier.ts, src/analyzers/risk-scoring.ts, src/cli/commands/workspace.ts, src/cli/mcp.ts, src/generators/pr-markdown-template.ts (+3 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/branch-work-analysis/context.md`

### Claude Config (`claude-config`)

Stores Claude Code editor settings and local overrides for the workspace.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/claude-config/context.md`

### CLI Operations (`cli-operations`)

CLI command implementations, output formatting, error handling, and credential/git helpers that form the user-facing interface of sourcevision.

- Files: 26
- Cohesion: 0.67, Coupling: 0.33
- Entry points: src/analyzers/manifest.ts, src/cli/commands/init.ts, src/cli/commands/pr-markdown.ts, src/cli/errors.ts, src/cli/output.ts (+1 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-operations/context.md`

### File Analysis Pipeline (`file-analysis-pipeline`)

Houses the four concrete analyzer implementations (callgraph, components, imports, inventory) that form the sequential analysis pipeline, alongside path utilities and route detection.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.48, Coupling: 0.52
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph.ts, src/analyzers/components.ts, src/analyzers/imports.ts, src/analyzers/inventory.ts, src/util/paths.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/file-analysis-pipeline/context.md`

### Schema Validation (`schema-validation`)

Contains the schema validation logic (src/schema/validate.ts) alongside its unit, integration, and e2e tests, forming a self-contained correctness boundary for analysis output formats.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Entry points: src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/schema-validation/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`test-fixtures`)

Static sample projects (a Remix app and a small TypeScript project) that serve as realistic analysis targets for e2e and integration tests.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/test-fixtures/context.md`

### Workspace Root (`workspace-root`)

Top-level project scaffolding: documentation, monorepo config, TypeScript project references, and build metadata.

- Files: 10
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/workspace-root/context.md`

### Zone Detection Engine (`zone-detection-engine`)

Implements Louvain community detection, zone hashing, and zone subdivision/size-policy logic to cluster files into stable architectural zones.

- Files: 6
- Cohesion: 0.29, Coupling: 0.71
- Entry points: src/analyzers/zone-hash.ts, src/analyzers/zones.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/zone-detection-engine/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/cli/output.ts` (14 imports)
- `src/util/sort.ts` (12 imports)
- `src/analyzers/claude-client.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/cli/errors.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/schema/data-files.ts` (10 imports)
- `src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyzers/zones.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/util/paths.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyzers/inventory.ts` (7 imports)
- `src/analyzers/token-usage.ts` (7 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 54 files)
- `@n-dx/llm-client` (used by 23 files)
- `typescript` (used by 5 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `zod` (used by 2 files)
- `ora` (used by 1 files)
- `pdfkit` (used by 1 files)

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- 16 entry points spanning 9 directories means there is no canonical import path into this zone; consumers import from arbitrary internal paths, making any file move a breaking change — consolidate exports behind a single index or public.ts to create a stable zone API surface [warning] (analysis-engine)
- enrich-config.ts (classified [config]) is co-located with domain services in analysis-engine; configuration files that change on deployment cadence should not share a zone with algorithmic services that change on feature cadence — extract to a dedicated config layer to decouple change rates [info] (analysis-engine)
- mcp.ts is a CLI command co-located with domain services in branch-work-analysis; CLI adapters must not share a zone boundary with domain logic they adapt — move mcp.ts to cli-operations to enforce CLI-above-domain layering within the zone, not just across zones [warning] (branch-work-analysis)
- pr-markdown-template.ts is a presentation-layer template embedded in a domain analysis zone; output templates have no domain responsibility and should reside in cli-operations or a dedicated output-rendering zone to prevent domain logic from acquiring a dependency on formatting details [warning] (branch-work-analysis)
- branch-work-collector.ts lives in cli-operations but collects domain data for branch-work-analysis; moving it to branch-work-analysis would eliminate a cross-layer import forcing analysis-engine to reach into the CLI zone for domain data collection logic [warning] (cli-operations)
- manifest.ts is an analysis pipeline artifact (analysis-engine output) placed inside cli-operations; if analysis-engine imports it, this single misplacement is a direct cause of the critical layer inversion — move manifest.ts into analysis-engine or a shared types layer [critical] (cli-operations)
- src/analyzers/index.ts is designated [entrypoint] but the zone still exports 15 cross-zone import edges to analysis-engine, meaning the barrel export is not functioning as a stable abstraction boundary — consumers import implementation files directly, rendering the entrypoint ineffective as an isolation mechanism. [warning] (file-analysis-pipeline)
- src/analyzers/route-detection.ts carries the [route-handler] archetype but performs static file-path analysis, not HTTP routing; this archetype mislabel causes tooling to misclassify a static analysis utility as an application-layer request handler — reclassify to [utility]. [warning] (file-analysis-pipeline)
- tests/e2e/cli-analyze.test.ts and tests/e2e/cli-serve.test.ts are CLI behavior tests misclassified into the schema-validation zone because they happen to import schema types; this makes the schema zone appear to own CLI e2e coverage and splits CLI test files across two zones — move both files to the CLI e2e test zone. [warning] (schema-validation)
- zone-hash.ts generates stable zone IDs consumed by both zone detection and output generation phases; enclosing it inside the zone-detection-engine zone forces output writers to import across a zone boundary into the detection subsystem rather than from a shared utility layer — extract to a dedicated utility zone. [warning] (zone-detection-engine)
- ... and 1 more

### Patterns

- Schema foundation types (v1.ts, schema/index.ts) are co-located inside branch-work-analysis rather than a dedicated foundation zone; consumers importing schema types inadvertently couple to the branch-work-analysis zone boundary, making schema extraction riskier over time [warning] (branch-work-analysis)
- prd-epic-resolver.ts references PRD/epic domain concepts inside the sourcevision CLI zone; audit whether this file imports from rex at runtime, which would violate the four-tier dependency hierarchy and the gateway pattern without a declared gateway module [warning] (cli-operations)
- src/schema/index.ts and src/schema/v1.ts function as a shared foundation layer (imported by 3–4 zones, zero outgoing imports) but are classified under a zone named 'schema-validation' dominated by test files — rename or restructure to reflect the foundation role and prevent the schema types from being treated as validation-layer internals. [warning] (schema-validation)
- Fixture projects spanning 5 directories with no centralized entry point risk being partially excluded by glob-based ignore rules — verify that sourcevision's own manifest ignore patterns do not accidentally suppress fixture files needed for e2e test coverage. [warning] (test-fixtures)
- Zone-detection-engine has the worst cohesion/coupling ratio (0.29/0.71) at the smallest meaningful size (6 files) — this combination makes it the highest per-file refactoring risk in the codebase and the most urgent candidate for boundary stabilization. [warning] (zone-detection-engine)

### Relationships

- analysis-engine → cli-operations (12 imports): domain layer importing from CLI layer is a hard layer inversion; analysis services must not depend on CLI command modules — extract shared types or utilities downward to break this coupling [critical] (analysis-engine)
- branch-work-analysis ↔ analysis-engine (30+7 crossings) is the dominant edge pair in the graph — 37 cross-zone imports between two zones signals that zone boundaries do not reflect real module seams; these zones likely share a common abstraction that should be extracted [warning] (branch-work-analysis)
- cli-operations receives 12 imports from analysis-engine while also sending 5 imports back — bidirectional coupling with a domain layer means cli-operations contains logic that the domain needs, which should instead live in a shared utility or types layer [critical] (cli-operations)
- src/util/paths.ts should be extracted to a dedicated utility zone; its presence in the file-analysis-pipeline zone obscures its true role as a cross-cutting shared utility and inflates this zone's apparent coupling. [warning] (file-analysis-pipeline)

### Observations

- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (analysis-engine)
- 16 entry points is unusually broad for a single zone; audit whether all exports need to be public or if the public API surface can be narrowed. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- High cohesion (0.66) and low coupling (0.34) indicate a well-bounded analysis domain — this is the healthiest zone in the batch. [info] (analysis-engine)
- claude-client.ts should proxy through @n-dx/llm-client to maintain the four-tier dependency hierarchy and avoid direct vendor coupling. [info] (analysis-engine)
- High coupling (0.58) — 30 imports target "analysis-engine" [warning] (branch-work-analysis)
- Coupling at 0.58 approaches the warning threshold (>0.6); monitor as new consumers of schema/v1.ts and data-files.ts are added. [info] (branch-work-analysis)
- Extracting schema/v1.ts and schema/data-files.ts into a dedicated schema-foundation zone would reduce coupling and clarify the dependency graph for downstream consumers. [info] (branch-work-analysis)
- The zone mixes schema foundation files (v1.ts, data-files.ts), branch-work services, and a test helper — low cohesion (0.42) reflects this mixed responsibility. [warning] (branch-work-analysis)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 2 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (claude-config)
- Zone contains only editor configuration — zero cohesion and coupling scores are expected and correct for this role. [info] (claude-config)
- ... and 29 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "analysis-engine" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analysis-engine)
- The zone name 'analysis-engine' is singular and bounded but the zone spans 9 directories with 16 entry points; rename or split the zone so the name truthfully describes its scope — a misleading name causes developers to underestimate blast radius when modifying files here [info] (analysis-engine)
- completion-reader.ts handles streaming completion I/O while its sibling files perform domain analysis — extract streaming/I/O adapters into a dedicated adapter layer below the analysis services to give I/O concerns a stable, separately evolvable home [info] (analysis-engine)
- Zone "branch-work-analysis" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (branch-work-analysis)
- The 'unit' alias used in cross-import tables for this zone is a residual name from a prior zone structure; update all documentation and tooling references to use the canonical ID 'branch-work-analysis' to prevent import tracing errors in future analysis passes [info] (branch-work-analysis)
- branch-work-analysis is the only zone with both cohesion below 0.5 and coupling above 0.5 — treat it as the highest-fragility zone in the codebase; prioritize extracting schema foundation types first (reduces coupling) and moving CLI artifacts out second (increases cohesion) before adding any new files to this zone [warning] (branch-work-analysis)
- workspace-aggregate.ts and workspace.ts (CLI) share a naming prefix implying a module family, but are governed by different zone boundaries with no enforced convention; document an explicit naming rule — domain aggregates use '-aggregate' suffix, CLI adapters use the bare noun — and verify all paired domain/CLI files follow it [info] (branch-work-analysis)
- Zone "cli-operations" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli-operations)
- export-pdf.ts introduces a binary rendering dependency (PDF) into cli-operations alongside text-output commands; if PDF generation pulls in a heavyweight renderer dependency, extract it to a separate output-renderers zone to prevent that dependency from being loaded for all CLI consumers that never use PDF export [info] (cli-operations)
- git-credential-helper.ts handles credential secrets inside the same zone as general CLI output commands; isolate it behind a CredentialHelper interface or move it to a dedicated security-utilities zone so that secret-handling logic has an explicit, auditable boundary and cannot be accidentally imported by non-security callers [warning] (cli-operations)
- ... and 22 more

| `tsconfig.json` | config |  | workspace-root |
| `tsconfig.tsbuildinfo` | config |  | workspace-root |
| `vitest.config.ts` | config |  | workspace-root |

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
